programme.title: Tina
programme.desc: Born Anna Mae Bullock in 1939, Tina Turner has sold in excess of 100 million records and earned numerous plaudits including Grammy awards and induction into the Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame with her first husband, Ike Turner. She was the first woman to grace the cover of Rolling Stone magazine and dabbled in acting including a memorable performance opposite Mel Gibson in Mad Max Beyond Thunderdome.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: Investigating the unsolved 2015 murder of John Palmer, who was nicknamed Goldfinger after his involvement in melting down gold from the 1983 Brink's-Mat robbery. Palmer was found dead in his Essex garden and while police originally believed he died of natural causes, they eventually confirmed the case was being treated as a professional hit.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: The search for John Palmer's killer leads to Tenerife, where he built a timeshare empire. Undercover stings and Russian mob ties expose the scale of his power.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: A professional hitman is suspected in John Palmer's murder, with evidence pointing to a cartel and an Eastern European enforcer.

programme.title: Costa Concordia: Chronicle of a Disaster
programme.desc: Documentary told from different perspectives of the sinking of the cruise ship, examining why it ran aground and how the chaotic evacuation took place.

programme.title: The Great Horsemeat Scandal
programme.desc: A look at the major news story that broke in 2013, when it was found that meat unfit for human consumption had been smuggled into the human food chain and turned into cheap frozen meals for companies including Aldi, Tesco, Lidl and Burger King. With testimonies from whistleblowers, detectives and activists.

programme.title: Discovering: Diane Keaton
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the Academy Award-winning actress, who starred in Annie Hall, The Godfather and Marvin's Room.

programme.title: The Eighties
programme.desc: A look at the evolution of TV throughout the 1980s, from VCRs and remote controls to family sitcoms and prime-time soaps.

programme.title: Discovering: Morgan Freeman
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the American actor, the star of films including Driving Miss Daisy and The Shawshank Redemption and winner of an Oscar for Million Dollar Baby.

programme.desc: A look at the life and career of English director and cinematographer Nicolas Roeg, whose movies include Performance, Walkabout, Don't Look Now and The Man Who Fell to Earth.

programme.title: The Movies
programme.desc: The journey through American cinema continues with more from the 1990s, including Unforgiven, Heat, Fargo, Toy Story, Groundhog Day and Titanic.

programme.title: Janet Jackson
programme.desc: Following the 2004 Super Bowl controversy, Janet finds herself blacklisted. Refusing to accept defeat, friends and fans fight for her right to be recognised.

programme.title: Escobar by Escobar
programme.desc: The story behind the cursed Escobar empire, following the fortunes of Juan Pablo, who was the sole heir to the biggest drug dealer in history.

programme.title: Pantani: The Accidental Death of a Cyclist
programme.desc: Documentary about the rise and fall of Marco Pantani, the maverick Italian cyclist found dead less than six years after winning the Tour de France.

programme.title: Busby
programme.desc: A profile of former Manchester United manager Matt Busby, who transformed football through a career of great talent and tragedy. Featuring previously unseen archive footage and interviews with those who knew the man best.

programme.title: Terry Venables: A Man Can Dream
programme.desc: A look at the life and times of the football player-turned-manager, who died in November 2023, intertwined with the story of England's unforgettable run at Euro 96, with insight from players, friends and family.

programme.title: Murder at the Post Office
programme.desc: In 2010, the North Yorkshire village of Melsonby was left in shock when postmistress Diana Garbutt was found murdered in the post office she ran with her husband Robin. He claimed she'd been the victim of a violent intruder, but as detectives probed further, suspicion began to fall on him and he was convicted of her murder and sentenced to life in prison in 2011. programme.title: Murder at the Post Office
programme.desc: At trial, the prosecution alleges Robin was stealing from the Post Office and evidence from the Horizon computer system is a key part of the case against him.

programme.title: Murder at the Post Office
programme.desc: Robin is sentenced to 20 years, but key evidence is missing, with none of his DNA on the murder weapon and data from the Horizon system unable be trusted.

programme.title: Bobby
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating Bobby Moore, exploring his football career, including leading the England team to victory in the 1966 world cup, and the personal challenges he faced both on the pitch and in retirement. Featuring archive footage and contributions from family, friends and celebrity fans.

programme.title: Brazil 2002
programme.desc: Documentary following the Brazilian national team through the 2002 World Cup, which culminated in the country's fifth victory in the tournament.

programme.title: Predators
programme.desc: In the remote mountains of Patagonia, a female puma has just six months to teach its cubs how to survive in an increasingly competitive world.

programme.title: Colombia's Wild Coast
programme.desc: The remote island of Gorgona, once chosen by the Colombian government as a prison site due to its shark-infested seas and impenetrable jungle teeming with deadly snakes.

programme.title: Wild Rockies
programme.desc: A look at how survival in the forest means maintaining a delicate balance between growth and destruction - something the elk, moose and wolves know only too well.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: When a great horned owl arrives covered in oil, Dr Sherri puts it through a terrifying procedure to save its life. Meanwhile, boxes of young foxes need help.

programme.title: Superbeast
programme.desc: A celebration of nature's ultimate athletes as they survive, excel and dominate in Earth's toughest arenas, from apex predators to cunning creatures. Gorillas smash termite mounds to pieces, jaguars crack the skulls of caimans with apparent ease, and orcas hunt their prey as a team.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: The team races to contain a case of malaria brought in by orphaned infant Cesia, before assessing surrogate mum Sri's plan to help Bumi.

programme.title: Great Blue Wild

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife

programme.desc: The team prepares to introduce Kiki to fellow chimp Patricia, Oshine receives a special visitor from South Africa and Bueno has emergency treatment on several rotten teeth. Meanwhile, the keepers hear sad news about the world's oldest woolly monkey, Xuzy.
